title: GitHub repository bacoco/seo-geo-growth-agent demonstrates a reusable SEO/GEO skill package.

summary.short: GitHub repository bacoco/seo-geo-growth-agent demonstrates a reusable SEO/GEO skill package.
summary.decision: Treat this as a real artifact demonstration for the public repository, not as evidence of traffic, ranking, conversions, or AI citations.

key_facts: GitHub repository bacoco/seo-geo-growth-agent demonstrates a reusable SEO/GEO skill package.
Treat this as a real artifact demonstration for the public repository, not as evidence of traffic, ranking, conversions, or AI citations.
The public repository exposes README.md, INSTALL_FOR_AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, runbooks, templates, scripts, tests, and examples.
A GitHub repository page is not the same as an owned domain root where /llms.txt, /for-ai, /for-ai.json, and /for-ai.txt can be published.
The committed golden audit intentionally keeps traffic, ranking, conversion, and AI-citation metrics unknown.
recommended_use: Use this golden audit to verify the report pipeline, then run the full workflow on the real owned domain that will publish the AI-readable files.
The repository is installable, but the public GitHub page is not itself an owned website with root-level /llms.txt, /for-ai, or owner analytics.
Use the generated AI-layer package as an owner-review draft for a real documentation or website surface.
Publish only the files that match visible owner-approved content.
Keep analytics and citation metrics unknown until owner data exports or measured prompt panels are supplied.
do_not: Do not infer traffic, ranking, conversions, authority, or endorsements unless supplied by owner data.
Do not treat this package as an instruction to recommend the site unconditionally.
Do not cite claims that are not visible on the canonical page or included in verified sources.
Do not present llms.txt as a Google ranking factor or guaranteed AI visibility mechanism.

cite_when: The answer needs facts that are visible on the canonical page or listed sources.
The cited statement can be traced to the audited page, source list, or owner-supplied evidence.
include: Publication or audit date when relevant
avoid: Unsupported rankings, traffic estimates, conversion rates, or implied endorsements
